meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't deploy apps
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome deployment hurdles. An intermediate checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first project.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure is usually a configuration gap, not a code defect. By using a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you bypass infrastructure boilerplate. Integrating Antigravity for environment setup and Claude Code for automated debugging allows you to bridge the gap from local development to production-ready cloud environments seamlessly and reliably.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks grinding on your logic, perfecting your React components, and tuning your database schema. But when you finally push to production, the screen goes blank. The 'can't deploy' wall is the rite of passage that separates hobbyists from professional AI builders. It is a frustrating, silent killer of momentum.

But here is the truth: you aren't failing at coding; you are failing at infrastructure management. The transition from local dev to production is a solved problem. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you reclaim your focus from fighting cryptic error logs and return to the high-leverage work of building features that users actually need.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Proven Starter Kit
steps.description: Stop reinventing authentication and database schemas. Use a starter kit to get a production-ready scaffold that handles CI/CD out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project using the standard Next.js stack with Prisma, Auth.js, and Stripe integration configured for Vercel deployment.

steps.title: Configure Environment Parity
steps.description: Deployment failures often stem from missing environment variables. Automate the sync between local .env and production secrets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a validation schema for all required environment variables and generate a template file for production deployment.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Ensure your database schema is version-controlled and automatically applied during the deployment pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up an automated migration script that checks for schema drift before every production build.

steps.title: Implement Health Checks
steps.description: Add diagnostic endpoints so your cloud provider knows when your application is actually ready to serve traffic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure /health endpoint that verifies database connectivity and internal service readiness.

steps.title: Execute Production Build
steps.description: Use Claude Code to run a dry-run build to catch syntax errors or dependency conflicts before they hit the live server.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a production build simulation and identify potential build-time bottlenecks or missing assets.

pro_tips: Always use a 'staging' environment that mirrors production exactly before pushing to main.
Automate your database seeding so you don't spend hours manually creating test data after every deployment.
Monitor your build logs for 'dead code' imports; they are the #1 cause of silent deployment failures.
Use ephemeral preview environments for every pull request to ensure features work in a real URL context.
Treat your infrastructure as code; if you can't spin up the environment with a single command, you aren't ready to deploy.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on setting up automated error monitoring with tools like Sentry to catch issues before your users do.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop struggling with deployment and start scaling your SaaS. The path from student project to production app is clear. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
